title: viola nemoralis subsp. nemoralis
intro: Viola nemoralis subsp. nemoralis, also known as the woodland violet, is a perennial plant species native to Europe. It is a member of the violet family (Violaceae) and is characterized by its heart-shaped leaves and delicate purple flowers. The medicinal properties of Viola nemoralis subsp. nemoralis have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including anxiety, insomnia, and digestive problems, due to its flavonoid and phenolic content. The herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to promote relaxation, soothe skin irritations, and support overall well-being.
